structure_description: 14-section policy + regulation deep dive on 1 country × 1 industry/topic. Front matter (cover/methodology/TOC) → 2-page executive summary → divider opens the regulatory chapter → policy history → framework + regulators overview → policy timeline centerpiece → recent changes deep dives → pending changes + lobby positions → compliance cost + winner/loser mapping → ASEAN peer comparison → methodology endnote. Lower data-viz density than market_analysis; the policy_timeline page is the structural and visual centerpiece.

sections.purpose: Report cover — title (e.g. 'EV subsidy regime — Vietnam 2026'), country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag
sections.notes: No content generation needed — pure template fill from parsed topic metadata. Title should foreground the policy regime, not the industry size.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview: left col = primary research approach (gazette tracking, ministry FOI requests, industry counsel interviews); right col = secondary corpus + source tagging conventions
sections.notes: Left-column items should foreground policy-specific sources (national gazette, ministry circulars, parliamentary records) rather than the generic primary-research framing used in market_analysis.

sections.purpose: Table of contents — generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Two-page executive summary. Page 1 = 4 callouts (regime overview metric, biggest recent change, dominant winner industry, compliance cost order-of-magnitude) + 2-col narrative + 1 small chart (e.g. cumulative regulation count or one headline metric over time). Page 2 = 5 strategic implications cards — IMPORTANT: label this grid 'Strategic implications for operators', not the market_analysis default 'for market participants'.
sections.char_budget_hint: callout num_max=12 chars, callout label_max=30, change-line_max=38, narrative_left total_chars_max=1200, implications cards body_max=360 chars each
sections.voice_note: Page 1 thesis should name the regime + its most decisive recent shift in one sentence. Winner callout should name an industry/sub-segment, not a company (no firm names in exec summary).

chart_type_options.name: 5-year trend line
chart_type_options.use_when: Cumulative regulation count or a headline regime metric has 5+ annual data points

chart_type_options.name: 3-year trend line
chart_type_options.use_when: Headline metric available only across the last 3 years (e.g. post-reform window)

chart_type_options.name: Stacked bar share split
chart_type_options.use_when: Headline metric decomposes into status/category bands across years (active vs pending vs sunset)

sections.purpose: Dark-mode chapter break — single thesis statement about the regime's trajectory (e.g. 'Tightening, with a one-window carve-out for strategic investors.')
sections.notes: Only divider in this blueprint — sets the tone for the substantive regulatory analysis that follows. Keep thesis ≤ 90 chars.

sections.purpose: 1-2 paragraphs of historical context (what triggered the current regime, prior policy posture, key reform inflection) + small chart showing cumulative regulation count, enforcement-action count, or scope-of-coverage expansion over 5-10 years. Chart is supporting, not centerpiece.
sections.char_budget_hint: narrative ≤ 1000 chars; chart side ≤ 8 data points; chart subtitle includes time period
sections.voice_note: Open with the structural framing — 'The regime traces to {{anchor_event}} {{anchor_year}}' — not a date-stamped chronological recap. Reader needs the through-line, not the timeline (the timeline lives in section 08).

chart_type_options.name: 5-year (or longer) trend line
chart_type_options.use_when: Cumulative regulation count, enforcement actions, or scope coverage has 5+ annual data points across the regime window

chart_type_options.name: Stacked bar share split
chart_type_options.use_when: Historical scope decomposes into category bands (e.g. statutes by ministry, enforcement by sector) over years

chart_type_options.name: Horizontal segment bar
chart_type_options.use_when: History is best read as a ranked snapshot of prior policy postures or reform inflection points rather than a continuous series

sections.purpose: Two-block page: top half = key statutes table (statute/decree name, year, scope, status: active/superseded/pending) — 6-10 rows; bottom half = compact regulator map (ministries + agencies with enforcement role, 4-6 boxes showing parent → enforcement-arm relationships). No narrative paragraphs — let the table + diagram carry the page.
sections.char_budget_hint: statute name ≤ 50 chars; scope ≤ 80 chars; regulator box label ≤ 30 chars; status badge from controlled vocab
sections.voice_note: Statute names use official short-form + numeric ID (e.g. 'Decree 100/2024/ND-CP', 'Halal Product Assurance Law (Law 33/2014)'). Never paraphrase statute names — readers fact-check these.

chart_type_options.name: Regulator network / parent-enforcement diagram
chart_type_options.use_when: Ministries delegate to enforcement arms in a multi-node tree; relationships matter as much as the entities

chart_type_options.name: Horizontal statute-by-scope bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Regulator landscape is flat (4 or fewer agencies) and statute coverage breadth is the more useful read than the network

sections.purpose: CENTERPIECE PAGE. Horizontal SVG timeline with 5-8 dated markers across the regime's effective window (typically 5-15 years). Each marker = one policy event (statute passed, amendment, enforcement crackdown, scope expansion, sunset). Annotations include: date (YYYY-MM), short label (≤38 chars), 1-2 sentence body (≤110 chars), winner/loser indicator chip. Optional sidebar callout anchors the 'decisive shift' datapoint.
sections.char_budget_hint: 5-8 markers; label_max=38; body_max=110; sidebar callout follows standard callout caps (num=12, label=30, change-line=38)
sections.voice_note: Markers ordered chronologically, oldest left. Winner/loser tags assigned per marker — neutral if outcome contested. Body sentence should explain the SHIFT the policy created, not just restate the policy text. Source tag required per marker.
sections.design_tradeoff_note: This page type does not exist in page_schemas.json yet — see manifest.yaml comment for proposed shape. If schema authoring slips, fall back to market_data_chart with a custom SVG timeline in the chart slot (lose the winner/loser chip rendering).

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Single timeline splits into 2 pages: page 1 holds the older 4 markers + opening framing sidebar; page 2 holds the more recent 4+ markers + the 'decisive shift' callout. Both pages keep the same horizontal_timeline shape so the visual centerpiece reads as one extended object across the spread.

chart_type_options.name: Horizontal policy timeline
chart_type_options.use_when: Always — this section's page_type fixes the visual shape; only this option is permitted

sections.purpose: Deep dive on 2-3 specific recent policy changes (last 18-24 months). One change per half-page or per page depending on depth. Each change covers: what changed, effective date, scope of operators affected, transition timeline, immediate market response. Light viz — possibly 1 chart total across both pages (e.g. licence-applications volume pre/post change).
sections.skip_if: fewer than 2 substantive policy changes in the last 24 months — collapse into a single 1-page section if only 1 change qualifies
sections.char_budget_hint: per-change narrative ≤ 900 chars; each change opens with bolded effective date + statute ID
sections.voice_note: Lead with what changed materially, not 'On {{date}}, the government issued…'. The date goes in the bolded opener; the analytical claim leads the sentence.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Baseline already 2 pages; on expand, each page holds one change at full depth (instead of 2 changes per page at half-depth). Page 1 = change A full narrative + pre/post chart; page 2 = change B full narrative + scope-of-operators-affected callout. A 3rd change collapses into a comparison row in section 11.

chart_type_options.name: Dual-axis pre/post combo
chart_type_options.use_when: Two related metrics (e.g. licence-application volume + approval rate) shift around the change date and both are needed to read the response

chart_type_options.name: 3-year pre/post trend line
chart_type_options.use_when: Single metric (e.g. licence-application volume) has clean monthly or quarterly data spanning the change

chart_type_options.name: Horizontal bar of affected operator counts
chart_type_options.use_when: Scope of change is best framed as a ranked list of operator categories affected, not as a time series

sections.purpose: What's in the pipeline + who's pushing/resisting. Repurposes forecast_outlook page structure: base/likely/stretch scenarios become 'as-drafted / industry-amended / further-tightened' policy scenarios. 2-3 lobby positions surfaced with named trade bodies or industry coalitions (NOT individual firm names — keep at association level). Comment-letter quotes OK if attributed.
sections.skip_if: no pending or proposed changes identifiable from research — collapse and skip this section, recompute page counter
sections.char_budget_hint: scenario card body ≤ 300 chars; lobby position blurb ≤ 200 chars per position
sections.voice_note: Use neutral language for lobby positions ('argues', 'has urged', 'objects to'), never moralising ('opposes' is fine; 'unfairly opposes' is not). Coalition naming uses official short-form.

chart_type_options.name: 3-year forward projection line
chart_type_options.use_when: Pending change has a quantifiable forward-looking metric (e.g. projected affected-operator count under each scenario)

chart_type_options.name: Scenario-impact horizontal bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Three scenarios are best compared on a single anchor metric (compliance cost, scope, lead time) without a time dimension

chart_type_options.name: Lobby position 2x2 (intensity x alignment)
chart_type_options.use_when: 4+ lobby positions surface and a quadrant frame (support/oppose vs high/low intensity) clarifies the field

sections.purpose: Combined page: top half = compliance cost analysis (cost-per-operator estimate, fixed vs variable split, year-1 vs steady-state). Bottom half = winner/loser industry mapping — 2-column visual (winners left, losers right) with 3-5 entries per side, each entry showing industry/segment + 1-line rationale + magnitude chip (high/medium/low impact).
sections.char_budget_hint: cost analysis narrative ≤ 600 chars; winner/loser entry rationale ≤ 80 chars each; magnitude chip from controlled vocab
sections.voice_note: Compliance costs tagged [estimate] unless a regulator-published impact assessment exists (then [secondary] with citation). Winner/loser entries name industries or operator types ('integrated EV OEMs with local assembly'), never individual firm names.

expand_to_2_pages_if.what_changes_on_expand: Page 1 focuses on compliance cost analysis at full depth: cost-per-operator estimate by operator size tier + fixed/variable decomposition chart + year-1 vs steady-state callout. Page 2 holds an expanded winner/loser map with 5+ entries per side, each gaining a 2-line rationale (instead of the 1-line cap on the single-page version) and a per-entry source tag.

chart_type_options.name: Fixed vs variable cost stacked bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Compliance cost decomposes cleanly into fixed/variable (or year-1/steady-state) bands across operator size tiers

chart_type_options.name: Cost-per-operator horizontal bar
chart_type_options.use_when: Cost varies sharply by operator type/size tier and a ranked horizontal read carries the page better than a decomposition

chart_type_options.name: Winner/loser impact heatmap
chart_type_options.use_when: Winner/loser mapping benefits from a 2-axis grid (e.g. industry x impact magnitude) instead of a 2-column list

sections.purpose: Repurposes competitive_structure layout: 4-card grid showing 3-4 ASEAN peer countries' approach to the same policy domain + sidebar narrative on convergence/divergence trajectory. Each card: country, regime-type label (e.g. 'Open / Restricted / Hybrid'), 1 anchor metric, 1-line characterisation. Sidebar = 2-paragraph commentary on where {{country}} sits in the regional spectrum and whether convergence is likely.
sections.skip_if: topic is a country-idiosyncratic regime with no meaningful ASEAN peer comparison (e.g. constitutional reform) — collapse and skip
sections.char_budget_hint: peer card characterisation ≤ 90 chars; anchor metric ≤ 12 chars + unit ≤ 8; sidebar narrative ≤ 900 chars total
sections.voice_note: Peer selection: prefer 3 economically-comparable ASEAN-6 members (ID/VN/TH/PH/MY/SG). Avoid including ASEAN+3 economies (CN/JP/KR) — those are reference points, not peers.

chart_type_options.name: ASEAN choropleth on the anchor metric
chart_type_options.use_when: Anchor metric is numeric and comparable across all peers; visual reads more clearly as a regional map than a 4-card grid

chart_type_options.name: Peer-ranked horizontal bar on anchor metric
chart_type_options.use_when: Anchor metric is numeric and the rank ordering of peers is the most useful read

chart_type_options.name: Regime-type 2x2 (openness x enforcement intensity)
chart_type_options.use_when: Peers cluster on two regime dimensions (e.g. openness vs enforcement intensity) and the quadrant frame outperforms a ranked list

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary] 15% / [secondary] 70% / [estimate] 15% target percentages), key anchors (which gazettes / ministry sources / trade associations were tracked), KIRA estimate methodologies disclosed (especially the compliance-cost triangulation method), contact + next-research footer
sections.notes: Required — policy briefs are especially scrutinised for source provenance. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report. Explicitly note 'No source-archive firms cited' (transparent anti-positioning).

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '0NN / NN' in page-header counter slot — total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section (e.g. both Recent-changes pages tagged 'Section 08 · Recent changes')
assembly_rules.divider_placement: Single dark divider opening the regulatory chapter (section 05). No further chapter dividers — the report is short enough that one tonal break suffices.
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if condition triggers, omit the section AND recompute page counter for all subsequent sections. Skippable sections: 09 (recent changes — collapse if <2 changes), 10 (pending changes — drop if none), 12 (cross-border — drop if regime is country-idiosyncratic). Minimum surviving section count: 11.
